brief_document.title: Dashboard app scaffold on the house frontend stack
brief_document.purpose: Stand up apps/aion-dashboard as a workspace member on the exact apps/web stack, so every later brief builds on a familiar, house-consistent foundation that the aion-server can serve as static assets.
brief_document.task: Create the apps/aion-dashboard Vite single-page application matching apps/web: React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char), shadcn/Radix primitives. Wire house scripts, a base-path-configurable build, the SPA entry, and the shadcn primitives directory. No application features yet — this is the shell of the project.

requirements.title: Workspace member and house scripts
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create apps/aion-dashboard as a workspace member whose package.json declares the same script set as apps/web (dev, build, preview, check, format, typecheck) and the same toolchain (React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome).
acceptance: apps/aion-dashboard/package.json exists, is registered in the workspace, and declares dev (vite), build (vite build), preview, check (biome check .), format, and typecheck (tsc --noEmit).
Dependencies match the apps/web stack versions (React 19, Vite 7, Tailwind v4, @biomejs/biome, TanStack Query); no alternate framework, styling system, or state library is introduced.
biome.json mirrors apps/web's config including the 100-char line width.
bun install resolves cleanly for the new workspace member.

requirements.title: Vite build with configurable base path and SPA entry
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL configure Vite to build a static SPA bundle and to accept a configurable base path so aion-server (AW) can serve the bundle under a sub-path. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide the SPA entry (index.html) and the React root mount (src/main.tsx).
acceptance: vite.config.ts uses the React and Tailwind v4 plugins and reads the base path from an env var (defaulting to '/') so AW can mount the bundle under a sub-path.
index.html is the SPA entry referencing src/main.tsx.
src/main.tsx mounts the React root into the document.
bun run build produces a static bundle in dist/.

requirements.title: shadcn/Radix UI primitive surface
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide the shadcn/Radix UI primitives the dashboard needs under src/components/ui, configured the same way apps/web configures shadcn.
acceptance: components.json (or equivalent shadcn config) is present and matches apps/web's conventions.
src/components/ui contains at least the primitives later briefs require (badge, select, table, skeleton, tooltip, button) sourced from shadcn/Radix.
A trivial render test mounting one primitive compiles and passes.
bun run check passes on the scaffold.

boundaries: SHALL NOT add any application feature (list, detail, live feed) — those are AU-004, AU-005, AU-009.
SHALL NOT define wire types or the REST/WebSocket client — AU-002, AU-003.
SHALL NOT define server endpoints, the WebSocket protocol, or asset hosting — that is cluster AW.
verification: bun run build produces a static bundle.
No lint-disable comments added to silence findings.

checklist.text: apps/aion-dashboard exists as a workspace member with package.json declaring house scripts (dev, build, check, format, typecheck) matching apps/web.
The stack matches apps/web: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char line width); no alternate framework, styling system, or state library is introduced.
vite.config.ts builds a static SPA bundle and supports a configurable base path so AW can serve it under a sub-path.
src/main.tsx mounts the React root and index.html is the SPA entry; bun run build produces a static bundle and tsc --noEmit passes.
shadcn/Radix UI primitives are available under src/components/ui and used for all interactive controls.

stories.text: As a frontend maintainer, I want the dashboard built on the same stack and conventions as apps/web so that I can work on it without learning a new toolchain.

constraints.text: Match the apps/web stack exactly: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char), shadcn/Radix primitives, TanStack Query. No new framework, styling system, or state library.

Wire types come from the house generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/). The dashboard never hand-defines a type aion-core/aion-proto owns and never hand-edits a generated file.

The dashboard consumes the AW API/WebSocket contract; it defines no server endpoint, no WebSocket protocol, and no asset-hosting mechanism. Not-yet-pinned contract details are isolated in the api module for a one-file change.
Feature-folder convention from apps/web: each feature is components/ + hooks/ (+ context/lib as needed) + types.ts + index.ts. Barrels re-export; logic lives in named files.
Server state goes through TanStack Query; live updates flow through the WebSocket manager and patch query caches or local view state. No bespoke fetch-into-useState for server data.
Every async surface renders explicit loading, empty, and error states. No surface spins indefinitely or blanks on failure.

The WebSocket manager reconnects with bounded backoff on drop and the active view resyncs (re-fetch history/query, or replay after last-seen sequence where supported). A connection indicator is always present; a dropped socket never leaves a silently stale view.
The event timeline is ordered strictly by event sequence number and de-duplicates events arriving via both history fetch and live stream.
Biome lint + format clean (bun run check) and tsc --noEmit clean. No lint-disable comments to silence findings.
Namespace selection scopes every query and every subscription; switching namespace re-scopes both. No unscoped query when a namespace is selected.

resolved_context.intention: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event history rendered as a timeline, and watch events stream live as they happen. This cluster builds the frontend only. It consumes the HTTP/gRPC list/query/history API and the WebSocket event stream that the server (cluster AW) exposes and hosts as static assets; it does not run the engine, persist anything, or define the API. It must match the existing house frontend stack (React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Biome + Bun, shadcn/Radix, TanStack Query, a singleton WebSocket manager with reconnect, generated types from Rust) exactly, so it reads as part of the same codebase as apps/web. When done, an operator pointed at a running aion-server can observe every workflow in every namespace, drill into any execution's full history, and watch events stream in real time — with production-grade loading, empty, error, and reconnection behaviour throughout.

provenance.quote: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event
